devanagari: श्रीरामचरितम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । भगवान् आत्मनात्मानं राम उत्तमकल्पकैः । सर्वदेवमयं देवं ईजे आचार्यवान् मखैः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrāmacaritam śrīśuka uvāca bhagavān ātmanātmānaṃ rāma uttamakalpakaiḥ sarvadevamayaṃ devaṃ īje ācāryavān makhaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The glorious Lord Rāma who was educated in his preceptor’s academy[1], worshipped his ownself, viz. the Supreme Lord (Viṣṇu) who represents all gods in his person, by performing sacrifices with excellent materials.[2]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
ācāryavān, cf.—ācāryavān purvṣo veda—Chāndogya up. 6.14.2

Footnote [2]:
Conducted by excellent sage like Vasiṣṭha—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: होत्रेऽददाद् दिशं प्राचीं ब्रह्मणे दक्षिणां प्रभुः । अध्वर्यवे प्रतीचीं वा उत्तरां सामगाय सः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: hotre adhvaryave pratīcīṃ vā uttarāṃ sāmagāya saḥ
translation: (When the sacrifice was concluded), the Lord donated as sacrificial fee the eastern direction (eastern part of his empire) to the Hotṛ (the sacrificial priest who recites the mantras from the Ṛgveda), the Southern quarter to the sacrificial priest called Brahmā, the Western part to the adhvaryu (reciter of Sūktas from the Yajurveda), northern direction (northern part of his empire) to the priest that sang Sāma hymns in the sacrifice.

devanagari: आचार्याय ददौ शेषां यावती भूस्तदन्तरा । मन्यमान इदं कृत्स्नं ब्राह्मणोऽर्हति निःस्पृहः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ācāryāya dadau śeṣāṃ yāvatī bhūstadantarā manyamāna idaṃ kṛtsnaṃ brāhmaṇo

translation: To his preceptor (Vasiṣṭha), he gave as grant the remaining portion of the land at the centre, considering a Brāhmaṇa who is devoid of any craving or desire, deserves all this.

devanagari: इत्ययं तदलङ्कार वासोभ्यामवशेषितः । तथा राज्ञ्यपि वैदेही सौमङ्गल्या अवशेषिता ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ityayaṃ tadalaṅkāra vāsobhyāmavaśeṣitaḥ tathā rājñyapi vaidehī saumaṅgalyā avaśeṣitā

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: In this way he was left with his clothes and ornaments (that were on his person), while the queen Sītā (princess of Videha) had only those ornaments which were essential for a lady whose husband is alive (the rest, she distributed to Brahmaṇa [Brāhmaṇa] ladies).

devanagari: ते तु ब्राह्मणदेवस्य वात्सल्यं वीक्ष्य संस्तुतम् । प्रीताः क्लिन्नधियस्तस्मै प्रत्यर्प्येदं बभाषिरे ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: te tu brāhmaṇadevasya vātsalyaṃ vīkṣya saṃstutam prītāḥ klinnadhiyastasmai pratyarpyedaṃ babhāṣire

translation: Perceiving this affection of the Lord who treated Brāhmaṇas as gods, the Brāhmaṇas were highly pleased and with their hearts overwhelmed with affection, returned all the gifts to him and said:

devanagari: अप्रत्तं नस्त्वया किं नु भगवन् भुवनेश्वर । यन्नोऽन्तर्हृदयं विश्य तमो हंसि स्वरोचिषा ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: aprattaṃ nastvayā kiṃ nu bhagavan bhuvaneśvara yanno

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: “Oh illustrious Lord! What is it that you have not given to us when entering into our hearts you dispel the darkness (of ignorance) by your effulgence, Oh ruler of the world.

devanagari: नमो ब्रह्मण्यदेवाय रामायाकुण्ठमेधसे । उत्तमश्लोकधुर्याय न्यस्तदण्डार्पिताङ्घ्र्यये ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: namo brahmaṇyadevāya rāmāyākuṇṭhamedhase uttamaślokadhuryāya nyastadaṇḍārpitāṅghryaye
translation: We bow down to you, Oh Rāma who regard Brāhmaṇas as gods, and whose intelligence (i.e. knowledge) is boundless and unrestricted. You are the leader of those possessing excellent renown. Your feet are being meditated upon by sages and recluses”.

devanagari: कदाचित् लोकजिज्ञासुः गूढो रात्र्यामलक्षितः । चरन् वाचोऽश्रृणोद् रामो भार्यां उद्दिश्य कस्यचित् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: kadācit lokajijñāsuḥ gūḍho rātryāmalakṣitaḥ caran vāco
translation: Once upon a time, while Rāma went about in disguise and unnoticed at night, with a desire to know the pulse of the public, he overheard somebody speaking with reference to his queen Sītā.

devanagari: नाहं बिभर्मि त्वां दुष्टां असतीं परवेश्मगाम् । स्त्रैणो हि बिभृयात् सीतां रामो नाहं भजे पुनः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ bibharmi tvāṃ duṣṭāṃ asatīṃ paraveśmagām straiṇo hi bibhṛyāt sītāṃ rāmo nāhaṃ bhaje punaḥ
translation: “I would not accept you a wicked, unchaste woman who lived in the house of a stranger. Rāma, an exorbitant doter of that woman Sītā, might be retaining her, but I am not Rāma to have you again.”

devanagari: इति लोकाद् बहुमुखाद् दुराराध्यादसंविदः । पत्या भीतेन सा त्यक्ता प्राप्ता प्राचेतसाश्रमम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti lokād bahumukhād durārādhyādasaṃvidaḥ patyā bhītena sā tyaktā prāptā prācetasāśramam
translation: In this way, Sītā was abandoned by her husband who was afraid of the myriad-tongued, ignorant, unplacative public. She arrived at the hermitage of Vālmīki, the son of Pracetas.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां नवमस्कन्धे एकादशोऽध्यायः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ navamaskandhe ekādaśo
translation: Then being pregnant, in due course, she gave birth to twins—sons who came to be called Kuśa and Lava. The sage Vālmīki performed their purificatory rites (jātakarma).

devanagari: अन्तर्वत्‍न्यागते काले यमौ सा सुषुवे सुतौ । कुशो लव इति ख्यातौ तयोश्चक्रे क्रिया मुनिः । अङ्गदश्चित्रकेतुश्च लक्ष्मणस्यात्मजौ स्मृतौ । ...
english_devnagari: antarvat‍nyāgate kāle yamau sā suṣuve sutau kuśo lava iti khyātau tayoścakre kriyā muniḥ aṅgadaścitraketuśca lakṣmaṇasyātmajau smṛtau takṣaḥ puṣkala ityāstāṃ bharatasya mahīpate

translation: Lakṣmaṇa had two sons known as Aṅgada and Citraketu (Candraketu, Cakraketu), and Bharata’s sons were Takṣa and Puṣkala, Oh protector of the earth.

devanagari: सुबाहुः श्रुतसेनश्च शत्रुघ्नस्य बभूवतुः । गन्धर्वान् कोटिशो जघ्ने भरतो विजये दिशाम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: subāhuḥ śrutasenaśca śatrughnasya babhūvatuḥ gandharvān koṭiśo jaghne bharato vijaye diśām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The sons of Śatrughna were Subāhu and Śrutasena. During the course of the conquest of directions (i.e. of the world) Bharata slew Gandharvas by tens of millions and collecting their wealth, he handed it over to the king Rāma. Śatrughna slew the demon Lavaṇa[3], the son of Madhu and built up a city called Mathurā, on the site of Madhuvana.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Although Bhāgavata Purāṇa calls him a demon, it means an unfriendly king, for bis father Madhu was not a demon (vide infra 9.24.5).

devanagari: तदीयं धनमानीय सर्वं राज्ञे न्यवेदयत् । शत्रुघ्नश्च मधोः पुत्रं लवणं नाम राक्षसम् । हत्वा मधुवने चक्रे मथुरां नाम वै पुरीम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tadīyaṃ dhanamānīya sarvaṃ rājñe nyavedayat śatrughnaśca madhoḥ putraṃ lavaṇaṃ nāma rākṣasam hatvā madhuvane cakre mathurāṃ nāma vai purīm

translation: The sons of Śatrughna were Subāhu and Śrutasena. During the course of the conquest of directions (i.e. of the world) Bharata slew Gandharvas by tens of millions and collecting their wealth, he handed it over to the king Rāma. Śatrughna slew the demon Lavaṇa[3], the son of Madhu and built up a city called Mathurā, on the site of Madhuvana.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Although Bhāgavata Purāṇa calls him a demon, it means an unfriendly king, for bis father Madhu was not a demon (vide infra 9.24.5).

devanagari: मुनौ निक्षिप्य तनयौ सीता भर्त्रा विवासिता । ध्यायन्ती रामचरणौ विवरं प्रविवेश ह ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: munau nikṣipya tanayau sītā bhartrā vivāsitā dhyāyantī rāmacaraṇau vivaraṃ praviveśa ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sai class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sai class 1 verb]

translation: Sītā who was forsaken by her husband, entrusted both of her sons to the sage (Vālmīki), and meditating upon the feet of Rāma, she entered into the bowels of the earth—so goes the tradition.

devanagari: तर् श्रुत्वा भगवान् रामो रुन्धन्नपि धिया शुचः । स्मरंस्तस्या गुणान् तान् तान् नाशक्नोत् रोद्धुमीश्वरः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: tar śrutvā bhagavān rāmo rundhannapi dhiyā śucaḥ smaraṃstasyā guṇān tān tān nāśaknot roddhumīśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rudh class 7 verb], [vocative single from √ rudh class 7 verb]
[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: [4] Hearing of that news (about Sītā) and remembering of her various excellent virtues, (even) that glorious Lord Rāma was not able to restrain his grief, despite his attempts (to suppress it) by force of reason.

devanagari: स्त्रीपुंप्रसङ्ग एतादृक् सर्वत्र त्रासमावहः । अपीश्वराणां किमुत ग्राम्यस्य गृहचेतसः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: strīpuṃprasaṅga etādṛk sarvatra trāsamāvahaḥ apīśvarāṇāṃ kimuta grāmyasya gṛhacetasaḥ
translation: The attachment between men and women in this way brings (in its train) fear and affliction everywhere—even in the case of rulers of the world. What needs be said of ordinary persons whose minds are fixed on their house-hold (or of a common house-holder whose mind is attached to his house and property).

devanagari: तत ऊर्ध्वं ब्रह्मचर्यं धारयन् अजुहोत् प्रभुः । त्रयोदशाब्दसाहस्रं अग्निहोत्रं अखण्डितम् ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tata ūrdhvaṃ brahmacaryaṃ dhārayan ajuhot prabhuḥ trayodaśābdasāhasraṃ agnihotraṃ akhaṇḍitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: Thereafter the Lord (Rāma) led a life of strict continence, and continued to offer oblations to the sacrificial fire, for thirteen thousand years, without any interruption.

devanagari: स्मरतां हृदि विन्यस्य विद्धं दण्डककण्टकैः । स्वपादपल्लवं राम आत्मज्योतिरगात् ततः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: smaratāṃ hṛdi vinyasya viddhaṃ daṇḍakakaṇṭakaiḥ svapādapallavaṃ rāma ātmajyotiragāt tataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[accusative single from √ vidh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vidh class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vidh class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vidh class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vyadh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ vyadh class 4 verb]
[imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Leaving behind him in the heart of his devotees who meditate upon his foliage-like tender feet which were (once) pricked with thorns in the Daṇdaka forest, Rāma then repaired to his own self-luminous region.

devanagari: नेदं यशो रघुपतेः सुरयाच्ञयात्त । लीलातनोरधिकसाम्यविमुक्तधाम्नः । रक्षोवधो जलधिबन्धनमस्त्र पूगैः । किं तस्य शत्रुहनने कपयः सहायाः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: nedaṃ yaśo raghupateḥ surayācñayātta līlātanoradhikasāmyavimuktadhāmnaḥ rakṣovadho jaladhibandhanamastra pūgaiḥ kiṃ tasya śatruhanane kapayaḥ sahāyāḥ
translation: Rāma assumed this (human) form as a sport at the request of gods. His prowess (or essential nature) is free from (i.e. has no) equals or superiors. (Glorification of feats like) the destruction of Rākṣasas with multitudes of missiles or the construction of a bridge across the sea are no great praise in the case of Rāma. (It was only his sportive action). Were the monkeys his real helpmates in exterminating the enemies? (No).

devanagari: यस्यामलं नृपसस्सु यशोऽधुनापि । गायन्त्यघघ्नमृषयो दिगिभेन्द्रपट्टम् । तं नाकपालवसुपालकिरीटजुष्ट । पादाम्बुजं रघुपतिं शरणं प्रपद्ये ॥ २१ ...
english_devnagari: yasyāmalaṃ nṛpasassu yaśo gāyantyaghaghnamṛṣayo digibhendrapaṭṭam taṃ nākapālavasupālakirīṭajuṣṭa pādāmbujaṃ raghupatiṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]
[nominative single from √ paṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ paṭ class 1 verb]
[vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: I seek asylum with Rāma, the chief of Raghus, as my protector, whose pure glory efficacious enough to destroy all sins and serving as decorative ornaments to the elephants supporting the cardinal points (i.e. has spread all over the world)—is sung even today by sages (like Mārkaṇḍeya) in royal courts (as that of Yudhiṣṭhira), and whose lotus-like feet are adored by the crowns of the rulers of the celestial world (e.g. Indra), and Lord of wealth (Kubera) or by Protectors of the earth).

devanagari: स यैः स्पृष्टोऽभिदृष्टो वा संविष्टोऽनुगतोऽपि वा । कोसलास्ते ययुः स्थानं यत्र गच्छन्ति योगिनः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa yaiḥ spṛṣṭo kosalāste yayuḥ sthānaṃ yatra gacchanti yoginaḥ
translation: All the residents of the Kosai country by whom he was touched, seen, seated (by the side) or followed, attained to that region (Mokṣa) where persons expert in yoga go.

devanagari: पुरुषो रामचरितं श्रवणैरुपधारयन् । आनृशंस्यपरो राजन् कर्मबन्धैः विमुच्यते ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: puruṣo rāmacaritaṃ śravaṇairupadhārayan ānṛśaṃsyaparo rājan karmabandhaiḥ vimucyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: [5] Oh King! A person who treasures up in his mind the story of Rāma, (heard) through his ears, and exercises control over his passions, becomes free from the bondage of Karmas (and is liberated from Saṃsāra).
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Padaratnāvalī: ‘By merely listening to the name of Rāma, one becomes free from the bondage of karma. He quotes the following verse indicating how the whole world was imbued with Rāma, when Rāma was ruling:
rāmo rāmo rāma iti sarveṣām abhavattadā /
sarvo rāma-mayo loko yadā rāmas tva pālayat //
This verse is quoted by Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa also.

devanagari: श्रीराजोवाच । कथं स भगवान् रामो भ्रातॄन् वा स्वयमात्मनः । तस्मिन् वा तेऽन्ववर्तन्त प्रजाः पौराश्च ईश्वरे ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrājovāca kathaṃ sa bhagavān rāmo bhrātṝn vā svayamātmanaḥ tasmin vā te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The King (Patīkṣit) asked:
How did the glorious Lord Rāma behave with his brothers and relatives? How did he conduct himself? How did they (his kith and kin), his subjects and citizens of Ayodhyā (reciprocally) behave with their ruler?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । अथादिशद् दिग्विजये भ्रातॄन् त्रिभुवनेश्वरः । आत्मानं दर्शयन् स्वानां पुरीमैक्षत सानुगः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca athādiśad digvijaye bhrātṝn tribhuvaneśvaraḥ ātmānaṃ darśayan svānāṃ purīmaikṣata sānugaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]
[imperfect middle third single], [aorist middle third plural]

translation:
Śrī Śuka replied:
When the coronation was over, Rāma, the sovereign ruler of the three worlds, directed his brothers to conquer (and protect) all the four quarters (i.e. countries all over the world). He granted audience to all his people, and looked after the capital city along with his attendants.

devanagari: आसिक्तमार्गां गन्धोदैः करिणां मदशीकरैः । स्वामिनं प्राप्तमालोक्य मत्तां वा सुतरामिव ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: āsiktamārgāṃ gandhodaiḥ kariṇāṃ madaśīkaraiḥ svāminaṃ prāptamālokya mattāṃ vā sutarāmiva

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ mad class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ mad class 4 verb]

translation: With its streets besprinkled with water perfumed with sandal etc. and with fragrant drops of ichor exuded from the temples of elephants in rut, the city appeared to be exceedingly intoxicated with rapturous joy[6] at the return of its master.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
v.l. Vāśita-gāmiva—like a cow overwhelmed with passion

devanagari: प्रासादगोपुरसभा चैत्यदेवगृहादिषु । विन्यस्तहेमकलशैः पताकाभिश्च मण्डिताम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: prāsādagopurasabhā caityadevagṛhādiṣu vinyastahemakalaśaiḥ patākābhiśca maṇḍitām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ maṇḍ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ maṇḍ ]

translation: It was beautified with gold domes (lit. pitchers) set on the crests of mansions, towers, assemblies, shrines, temples as well as with flags.

devanagari: पूगैः सवृन्तै रम्भाभिः पट्टिकाभिः सुवाससाम् । आदर्शैरंशुकैः स्रग्भिः कृतकौतुकतोरणाम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: pūgaiḥ savṛntai rambhābhiḥ paṭṭikābhiḥ suvāsasām ādarśairaṃśukaiḥ sragbhiḥ kṛtakautukatoraṇām
translation: (The city was decorated) with (auspicious) arches erected with banana trees surrounded by (branches of) arecanut trees, (or decorations consisting of bunches of fruits and plantains) banners of fancy cloth, mirrors, canopies and wreaths of flowers.

devanagari: तं उपेयुस्तत्र तत्र पौरा अर्हणपाणयः । आशिषो युयुजुर्देव पाहीमां प्राक् त्वयोद्धृताम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ upeyustatra tatra paurā arhaṇapāṇayaḥ āśiṣo yuyujurdeva pāhīmāṃ prāk tvayoddhṛtām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active third plural], [perfect active third plural]

translation: With articles of worship (and presents) in their hands, citizens approached him at various places and conferred blessings on him and prayed, “Be pleased to protect the earth which you lifted up formerly (in your boar-incarnation)”.

devanagari: ततः प्रजा वीक्ष्य पतिं चिरागतं । दिदृक्षयोत्सृष्टगृहाः स्त्रियो नराः । आरुह्य हर्म्याण्यरविन्दलोचनं । अतृप्तनेत्राः कुसुमैरवाकिरन् ॥ ३०...
english_devnagari: tataḥ prajā vīkṣya patiṃ cirāgataṃ didṛkṣayotsṛṣṭagṛhāḥ striyo narāḥ āruhya harmyāṇyaravindalocanaṃ atṛptanetrāḥ kusumairavākiran

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ir class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ ir class 6 verb]

translation: Perceiving (hearing) that their Lord had arrived after a lapse of a long period, his subjects—both men and women—left their household duties, and with a desire to see him directly, climbed up to the tops of their houses, but though their eyes remained unsatisfied with viewing the lotuseyed Rāma, they showered him with flowers.

devanagari: अथ प्रविष्टः स्वगृहं जुष्टं स्वैः पूर्वराजभिः । अनन्ताखिलकोषाढ्यं मनर्घ्योरुपरिच्छदम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: atha praviṣṭaḥ svagṛhaṃ juṣṭaṃ svaiḥ pūrvarājabhiḥ anantākhilakoṣāḍhyaṃ manarghyoruparicchadam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Thereafter, the Lord entered his own residence which was once occupied by his predecessor-kings. It was rich in unlimited treasures of every kind, and was furnished with invaluable articles of furniture, coverings etc. It was beautified with doors the thresholds of which were of coral, and with rows of pillars of Vaiḍūrya (cat’s-eye) gems, reflecting (mirror like) floors of emeralds and walls of shining crystals.

devanagari: विद्रुमोदुम्बरद्वारैः वैदूर्य स्तंभपङ्क्तिभिः । स्थलैर्मारकतैः स्वच्छैः भाजत्स्फटिकभित्तिभिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vidrumodumbaradvāraiḥ vaidūrya staṃbhapaṅktibhiḥ sthalairmārakataiḥ svacchaiḥ bhājatsphaṭikabhittibhiḥ
translation: Thereafter, the Lord entered his own residence which was once occupied by his predecessor-kings. It was rich in unlimited treasures of every kind, and was furnished with invaluable articles of furniture, coverings etc. It was beautified with doors the thresholds of which were of coral, and with rows of pillars of Vaiḍūrya (cat’s-eye) gems, reflecting (mirror like) floors of emeralds and walls of shining crystals.

devanagari: चित्रस्रग्भिः पट्टिकाभिः वासोमणिगणांशुकैः । मुक्ताफलैश्चिदुल्लासैः कान्तकामोपपत्तिभिः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: citrasragbhiḥ paṭṭikābhiḥ vāsomaṇigaṇāṃśukaiḥ muktāphalaiścidullāsaiḥ kāntakāmopapattibhiḥ
translation: It was decorated with garlands of various colours and kinds, with banners and buntings, with cloths, gems canopies and pearls brilliant like intelligence or at places the columns of vaiḍūrya (cat’s eye)were decorated with brilliant pearls and equipped with all beautiful covetable objects. It was full of fragrance of incenses and lights and was decorated with flowers. It was provided with the services of men and women who being beautiful like gods, enhanced the charm of ornaments (they put on) themselves.

devanagari: धूपदीपैः सुरभिभिः मण्डितं पुष्पमण्डनैः । स्त्रीपुम्भिः सुरसङ्काशैः जुष्टं भूषणभूषणैः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: dhūpadīpaiḥ surabhibhiḥ maṇḍitaṃ puṣpamaṇḍanaiḥ strīpumbhiḥ surasaṅkāśaiḥ juṣṭaṃ bhūṣaṇabhūṣaṇaiḥ

translation: It was decorated with garlands of various colours and kinds, with banners and buntings, with cloths, gems canopies and pearls brilliant like intelligence or at places the columns of vaiḍūrya (cat’s eye)were decorated with brilliant pearls and equipped with all beautiful covetable objects. It was full of fragrance of incenses and lights and was decorated with flowers. It was provided with the services of men and women who being beautiful like gods, enhanced the charm of ornaments (they put on) themselves.

devanagari: तस्मिन् स भगवान् रामः स्निग्धया प्रिययेष्टया । रेमे स्वारामधीराणां ऋषभः सीतया किल ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin sa bhagavān rāmaḥ snigdhayā priyayeṣṭayā reme svārāmadhīrāṇāṃ ṛṣabhaḥ sītayā kila

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ snih class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ snih class 4 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: It is reported that in that palace the glorious Lord Rāma, the chief of spiritually wise persons who find bliss in their own self, enjoyed himself with his affectionate darling Sītā.

devanagari: बुभुजे च यथाकालं कामान् धर्ममपीडयन् । वर्षपूगान् बहून् नृणां अभिध्यातांघ्रिपल्लवः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: bubhuje ca yathākālaṃ kāmān dharmamapīḍayan varṣapūgān bahūn nṛṇāṃ abhidhyātāṃghripallavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Rāma whose foliage-like tender feet are contemplated upon by men, enjoyed for a great number of years all the pleasures at the proper time, without causing any violation of righteousness.
